content: Add a new isCommunityWorkout boolean field to the planned workout model. This field marks workouts created by the Amp customer community. On the iOS app, community workouts should display a unique icon/badge on the workout preview screen to distinguish them from coach-created workouts. This is a cross-repo feature: backend schema + iOS UI.

content:
---

# PlannedWorkout Model — Comprehensive Research Report

## 1. GraphQL Schema Definitions

### Primary Source (Amplify AppSync Schema)
**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql` (lines 1–74)

This is the **source of truth** for the Amplify/AppSync-managed PlannedWorkout model. It uses Amplify directives (`@model`, `@auth`, `@index`, `@hasMany`, `@belongsTo`, `@resolver`, `@lambda`, `@default`, `@deprecated`).

### Apollo Server Schema (Federated)
**File:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql` (lines 1–55)

This is the **Apollo Server (federated subgraph)** version. It mirrors the Amplify schema but uses federation directives (`@key`, `@shareable`, `@unauth`, `@override`). It adds `updatedAt` and `calculatedPlannedBlocks` which are Apollo-only fields.

### Combined/Build Schema
**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema.graphql` (lines 2769–2843)

This is the **auto-generated combined schema** from all individual schema files. It includes `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` (line 2838) which is NOT in the source `schema/planned-workout.graphql` — it was added directly to the combined schema.

---

## 2. Full Field List on PlannedWorkout

### Persisted Fields (stored in DynamoDB)

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| `id` | `ID!` | ✅ | Primary key |
| `versionGroupID` | `String!` | ✅ | GSI: `byVersionGroupID` |
| `version` | `Int!` | ✅ | Default: `1` |
| `isLatest` | `BooleanValue!` | ✅ | Default: `TRUE`, GSI: `byIsLatest` |
| `isPublicAndLatest` | `String!` | ✅ | GSI: `byIsPublicAndLatestAndCreatedAt` (sort: `createdAt`) |
| `name` | `String!` | ✅ | |
| `time` | `TimeMinutes!` | ✅ | Enum: FIVE, TEN, FIFTEEN, TWENTY, THIRTY, FORTY_FIVE, SIXTY |
| `type` | `WorkoutType!` | ✅ | GSI: `byWorkoutType` |
| `isBodyweight` | `Boolean!` | ✅ | Native boolean |
| `estimatedDurationMillis` | `Int` | ❌ | |
| `difficulty` | `Difficulty!` | ✅ | Enum: BEGINNER, INTERMEDIATE, ADVANCED |
| `bodyRegions` | `[BodyRegion]` | ❌ | |
| `goals` | `[WorkoutGoal]` | ❌ | |
| `description` | `String` | ❌ | |
| `muscleGroups` | `[MuscleGroup!]!` | ✅ | Deprecated: "Use granularMuscleGroups instead" |
| `granularMuscleGroups` | `[GranularMuscleGroup!]` | ❌ | |
| `isOnMachineWorkout` | `Boolean` | ❌ | Native boolean, optional |
| `coverImage` | `UserAsset` | ❌ | |
| `thumbnail` | `UserAsset` | ❌ | |
| `isChallenge` | `BooleanValue` | ❌ | GSI: `byIsChallenge` (sort: `challengeType`) |
| `isGenerated` | `Boolean` | ❌ | Deprecated: "Use isAutoGenerated instead" |
| `isAutoGenerated` | `BooleanValue` | ❌ | Default: `FALSE`, GSI: `byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType` (sort: `type`) |
| `workoutSource` | `WorkoutSource!` | ✅ | GSI: `byWorkoutSource` (sort: `name`) |
| `originallyGenerated` | `Boolean` | ❌ | Deprecated: "Use workoutSource instead" |
| `challengeType` | `ChallengeType!` | ✅ | |
| `isAmpMode` | `BooleanValue` | ❌ | GSI: `byIsAmpMode` (sort: `isLatest`) |
| `portraitThumbnail` | `UserAsset` | ❌ | |
| `tips` | `[String]` | ❌ | Deprecated: "Not used anymore" |
| `benefits` | `[String]` | ❌ | Deprecated: "Not used anymore" |
| `isPublic` | `BooleanValue!` | ✅ | GSI: `byIsPublicAndCreatedAt` (sort: `createdAt`) |
| `createdBy` | `String!` | ✅ | GSI: `byCreatedBy`, `byCreatedByAndCreatedAt`, `byCreatedByAndIsPublic` |
| `isDraft` | `BooleanValue!` | ✅ | |
| `isLiveChallenge` | `BooleanValue!` | ✅ | GSI: `byIsLiveChallenge` |
| `workoutGenerationVersion` | `String` | ❌ | GSI: `byWorkoutGenerationVersion` |
| `aiGenerationType` | `AIGenerationType` | ❌ | Enum: GEN_AI, AI_COACH, QUICK_ACTIONS, WORKOUT_ANYWHERE, GEN_AI_V3 |
| `createdAt` | `AWSDateTime!` | ✅ | |
| `isArchived` | `BooleanValue` | ❌ | |
| `isCommunityWorkout` | `Boolean` | ❌ | Only in combined schema, not in source file |

### Computed/Resolved Fields (NOT stored in DynamoDB)

| Field | Type | Resolution |
|-------|------|-----------|
| `instructor` | `Instructor` | `@belongsTo` (Amplify) / resolved via `instructorPlannedWorkoutsId` |
| `isComplete` | `Boolean!` | `@resolver` → PerformedWorkoutTable / Apollo resolver |
| `isDemoWorkout` | `Boolean` | `@resolver` → WorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkoutTable |
| `isFavorite` | `Boolean` | `@lambda(name: "getIsFavorite")` / Apollo resolver |
| `specialWorkoutType` | `SpecialWorkoutType` | Pipeline resolver (3 data sources) |
| `creatorData` | `CreatorData` | `@resolver` → UserProfileTable |
| `supportedOperations` | `[WorkoutOperation]` | Pipeline resolver (3 data sources) |
| `plannedBlocks` | `[PlannedBlock]` | `@hasMany` connection |
| `categories` | `[WorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkout]` | `@hasMany` connection |
| `programConnections` | `[ProgramWorkoutConnection]` | `@hasMany` connection |
| `calculatedPlannedBlocks` | `ModelPlannedBlockConnection` | Apollo-only, takes `workoutParticipantIds` arg |

---

## 3. Boolean Field Patterns

There are **two distinct patterns** for boolean fields on PlannedWorkout:

### Pattern A: `BooleanValue` Enum (String-based)
Used when the field needs to be a **DynamoDB GSI hash key** (DynamoDB doesn't support Boolean type for index keys).

```graphql
enum BooleanValue {
    TRUE
    FALSE
}
```

**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/root.graphql` (lines 56–59)

**Fields using this pattern:**
- `isLatest: BooleanValue!` — GSI hash key
- `isPublic: BooleanValue!` — GSI hash key
- `isChallenge: BooleanValue` — GSI hash key
- `isAutoGenerated: BooleanValue` — GSI hash key
- `isAmpMode: BooleanValue` — GSI hash key
- `isDraft: BooleanValue!` — no GSI but follows the pattern
- `isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue!` — GSI hash key
- `isArchived: BooleanValue` — no GSI

**In DynamoDB:** Stored as string `"TRUE"` or `"FALSE"` (type `"S"`).

### Pattern B: Native `Boolean`
Used for simple boolean fields that are **NOT** GSI keys.

**Fields using this pattern:**
- `isBodyweight: Boolean!` — required, not indexed
- `isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean` — optional, not indexed
- `isGenerated: Boolean` — deprecated
- `originallyGenerated: Boolean` — deprecated
- `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` — optional, not indexed

### Pattern C: Computed Boolean (resolved at query time)
- `isComplete: Boolean!` — resolved from PerformedWorkout table
- `isDemoWorkout: Boolean` — resolved from WorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkout table
- `isFavorite: Boolean` — resolved via Lambda

**Key insight:** If a new boolean field needs to be used as a DynamoDB GSI key, use `BooleanValue` enum. If it's just a simple stored field, use native `Boolean`. If it's computed at query time, use `Boolean` with a `@resolver` or `@lambda` directive.

---

## 4. Resolver File Paths

### Apollo Server Resolvers (TypeScript)
**Directory:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `PlannedWorkout.calculatedPlannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | Computed blocks with participant data |
| `PlannedWorkout.creatorData.resolver.ts` | Creator profile data |
| `PlannedWorkout.instructor.resolver.ts` | Instructor lookup |
| `PlannedWorkout.isComplete.resolver.ts` | Checks PerformedWorkout table |
| `PlannedWorkout.isFavorite.resolver.ts` | Checks user favorites |
| `PlannedWorkout.plannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | Block connection |
| `PlannedWorkout.specialWorkoutType.resolver.ts` | Assessment/Welcome/Benchmark detection |
| `PlannedWorkout.supportedOperations.resolver.ts` | ARCHIVE/EDIT operations |
| `PlannedWorkout.type.resolver.ts` | WorkoutType with version compatibility |
| `PlannedWorkoutSearchRecord.instructor.resolver.ts` | Search record instructor |
| `PlannedWorkoutSearchRecord.isComplete.resolver.ts` | Search record completion |
| `PlannedWorkoutSearchRecord.isFavorite.resolver.ts` | Search record favorite |
| `PlannedWorkoutSearchRecord.specialWorkoutType.resolver.ts` | Search record special type |
| `Query.getPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | Get single workout |
| `Query.listPersonalPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | List user's workouts |
| `Query.queryPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | Search/filter workouts |
| `Mutation.createPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create workout with blocks |
| `Mutation.updatePlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update workout with blocks |
| `Mutation.createPersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create personal workout |
| `Mutation.updatePersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update personal workout |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutV3.resolver.ts` | V3 generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutForUserV2.resolver.ts` | V2 generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutLP.resolver.ts` | LP generation |
| `Mutation.applyQuickActionPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | Quick action modifications |
| `Mutation.applyAIChangesToPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | AI-driven modifications |

### AppSync VTL Resolvers (Amplify-managed)
**Directory:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/build/resolvers/`

CRUD resolvers: `Mutation.createPlannedWorkout.*`, `Mutation.updatePlannedWorkout.*`, `Mutation.deletePlannedWorkout.*`
Query resolvers: `Query.listPlannedWorkoutsByCreatedBy.*`, `Query.listPlannedWorkoutsByIsPublicAndCreatedAt.*`, `Query.listPlannedWorkoutsByIsLiveChallenge.*`, `Query.listPlannedWorkoutsByWorkoutSource.*`
Connection resolvers: `PlannedWorkout.plannedBlocks.*`, `PlannedWorkout.categories.*`, `PlannedWorkout.programConnections.*`, `PlannedWorkout.instructor.*`

### Terraform Resolver Definitions
**File:** `infra/env/module/resolvers/resolvers.tf` (lines 69–186, 269–286, 320–328)

---

## 5. Database Model / TypeScript Type

### Generated TypeScript Type (`@amp/API`)
**File:** `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` (lines 7831–7888)

```typescript
export type PlannedWorkout = Activity & {
  __typename?: 'PlannedWorkout';
  aiGenerationType?: Maybe<AiGenerationType>;
  benefits?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  bodyRegions?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<BodyRegion>>>;
  calculatedPlannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  categories?: Maybe<ModelWorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkoutConnection>;
  challengeType: ChallengeType;
  coverImage?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  createdAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  createdBy: Scalars['String']['output'];
  creatorData?: Maybe<CreatorData>;
  description?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  difficulty: Difficulty;
  estimatedDurationMillis?: Maybe<Scalars['Int']['output']>;
  goals?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutGoal>>>;
  granularMuscleGroups?: Maybe<Array<GranularMuscleGroup>>;
  id: Scalars['ID']['output'];
  instructor?: Maybe<Instructor>;
  instructorPlannedWorkoutsId?: Maybe<Scalars['ID']['output']>;
  isAmpMode?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isArchived?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isAutoGenerated?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isBodyweight: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isChallenge?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isComplete: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isDemoWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isDraft: BooleanValue;
  isFavorite?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isLatest: BooleanValue;
  isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue;
  isOnMachineWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isPublic: BooleanValue;
  isPublicAndLatest: Scalars['String']['output'];
  muscleGroups: Array<MuscleGroup>;
  name: Scalars['String']['output'];
  originallyGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  plannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  portraitThumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  programConnections?: Maybe<ModelProgramWorkoutConnectionConnection>;
  specialWorkoutType?: Maybe<SpecialWorkoutType>;
  supportedOperations?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutOperation>>>;
  thumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  time: TimeMinutes;
  tips?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  type: WorkoutType;
  updatedAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  version: Scalars['Int']['output'];
  versionGroupID: Scalars['String']['output'];
  workoutGenerationVersion?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  workoutSource: WorkoutSource;
};
```

### DynamoDB Table Name
**File:** `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/utils/env/table-names.ts` (line 62)
```typescript
PlannedWorkout = 'PlannedWorkout',
```

### DynamoDB Table Definition (Terraform)
**File:** `infra/env-once/module/appsync_generated/plannedworkout/main.tf` (lines 42–240)

Table name pattern: `PlannedWorkout-{appSyncApiId}-{env}`
Primary key: `id` (String)
Billing: PAY_PER_REQUEST

**GSIs (15 total):**
1. `byVersionGroupID` — hash: `versionGroupID`
2. `byIsLatest` — hash: `isLatest`
3. `byIsPublicAndLatestAndCreatedAt` — hash: `isPublicAndLatest`, sort: `createdAt`
4. `byWorkoutType` — hash: `type`
5. `byIsChallenge` — hash: `isChallenge`, sort: `challengeType`
6. `byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType` — hash: `isAutoGenerated`, sort: `type`
7. `byWorkoutSource` — hash: `workoutSource`, sort: `name`
8. `byIsAmpMode` — hash: `isAmpMode`, sort: `isLatest`
9. `byIsPublicAndCreatedAt` — hash: `isPublic`, sort: `createdAt`
10. `byCreatedBy` — hash: `createdBy`, sort: `createdAt`
11. `byCreatedByAndCreatedAt` — hash: `createdBy`, sort: `createdAt`
12. `byCreatedByAndIsPublic` — hash: `createdBy`, sort: `isPublic`
13. `byIsLiveChallenge` — hash: `isLiveChallenge`
14. `byWorkoutGenerationVersion` — hash: `workoutGenerationVersion`
15. `gsi-Instructor.plannedWorkouts` — hash: `instructorPlannedWorkoutsId`

---

## 6. How to Add a New Field — Pattern Summary

### Adding a simple stored Boolean field (no GSI needed):
1. Add to `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql` as `fieldName: Boolean`
2. Add to `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
3. Run `pnpm generate` to regenerate `API.ts`
4. The Amplify `@model` directive auto-generates `CreatePlannedWorkoutInput` and `UpdatePlannedWorkoutInput` with the new field

### Adding a BooleanValue field (for GSI indexing):
1. Add to schema as `fieldName: BooleanValue` with `@index(name: "byFieldName")`
2. Add DynamoDB attribute + GSI in the Terraform file
3. Run `pnpm generate`

### Adding a computed/resolved field:
1. Add to both schema files with appropriate type
2. Create a resolver file at `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/PlannedWorkout.fieldName.resolver.ts`
3. Register the resolver in the Apollo Server resolver map
4. For Amplify: add `@resolver` directive pointing to the data source

---

COMPLETION REPORT
STEPS_RAN:
- RESEARCH: PASS — Found all schema definitions, types, resolvers, and DynamoDB table definitions
FILES_MODIFIED:
- (none — research only)
BLOCKED: NO
name: task

content:
The research is complete. Here's a summary of all findings:

## Research Complete

I've thoroughly investigated the iOS workout preview screen and planned workout display patterns. Here are the key findings:

### PlannedWorkout Model
The core model has ~30+ properties including visual-display-relevant ones: `type` (10 workout types), `workoutSource` (generated/programBuilder/byo/quickSet), `aiGenerationType` (5 AI types), `challengeType`, `specialWorkoutType`, and boolean flags (`isNew`, `isFavorite`, `isComplete`, `isCommunityWorkout`, `isBodyweight`).

### Badge System
**LibraryBadgesView** is the central badge component with this display order:
1. Generated (AI sparkles badge)
2. Challenge
3. Community
4. New (hidden if generated or completed)
5. Favorite
6. Completed

**Notable gap**: `LibraryWorkoutCardView` (Explore tab) does NOT pass `isGenerated` — AI badges only appear on `LibraryWorkoutRowView`, `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell`, and recommendation cards.

### Visual Distinction Patterns
- **Workout types** are shown as text labels only (no type-specific icons on cards)
- **AI-generated** workouts use `GeneratedAIBadge` (cut-corner taupe badge with sparkles) or `.generated` badge style
- **Home carousel** uses separate `WorkoutCardView` with `.plannedWorkoutDay` (yellow) and `.activeRecoveryDay` (blue) badges
- **`aiGenerationType`** exists on the model but is NOT used for visual distinction in any view currently

### Navigation Flow
Home → `WorkoutPreviewContext` → `HomeCoordinator.presentWorkoutDetailsScreen()` → `WorkoutDetailsViewModel` with contexts for standard, AI, quick action, and AI quick action workouts.

```
COMPLETION REPORT
STEPS_RAN:
- SCHEMA_PULL: N_A (research-only task, no schema changes)
- COMPILE: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- LINT: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- UNIT_TESTS: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- SNAPSHOT_TESTS: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- MAESTRO: SKIPPED (research-only task, not a UI change)
- SCREENSHOTS: none
FILES_MODIFIED:
- (none - research only)
BLOCKED: NO
BLOCK_REASON: N/A
```

content.text:
Excellent research. Now I have a clear picture of both repos. Let me formulate the plan.

Key findings:
- **Backend**: `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` already exists in the combined schema but NOT in the source schema files. I need to add it to both source schema files and run `pnpm generate`.
- **iOS**: A `LibraryBadgesView` already exists with a badge system that includes a `.community` badge type. The field `isCommunityWorkout` may already be partially wired. I need to ensure it's fully connected from schema → queries → UI.

input.title: Add isCommunityWorkout field to planned workout model
input.summary: Add isCommunityWorkout Boolean to backend schema + display community badge on iOS workout preview.
input.plan:
# Implementation Plan: isCommunityWorkout Field

## Backend (RedefinedFitness)

### Step 1: Add field to Amplify AppSync schema
- **File**: `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` as an optional field (no GSI needed — simple stored boolean)

### Step 2: Add field to Apollo Server schema
- **File**: `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to match

### Step 3: Run `pnpm generate`
- This regenerates `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` and the combined schema
- Verify the field appears in the generated TypeScript types

### Step 4: Run `pnpm typecheck`
- Ensure no type errors

## iOS (amp-ios)

### Step 5: Pull updated schema to iOS
- Copy/update `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Schema.graphql` with the new field
- Ensure `isCommunityWorkout` is included in relevant GraphQL fragments/queries

### Step 6: Add isCommunityWorkout to workout model
- Update the Swift PlannedWorkout model to include the new field
- Wire it through from the GraphQL response to the view model

### Step 7: Display community badge on workout preview
- The `LibraryBadgesView` already has a `.community` badge type in its display order
- Ensure the badge is properly shown when `isCommunityWorkout == true`
- The badge should appear on workout preview/details screens

### Step 8: Build and verify
- Run `xcodebuild` to verify compilation
- Run relevant unit/snapshot tests

## Test Plan
- [ ] Backend: `pnpm typecheck` passes with new field
- [ ] iOS: Project compiles with new field wired through
- [ ] iOS: Community badge displays when isCommunityWorkout is true
- [ ] Run relevant snapshot/unit tests for badge views

# Implementation Plan: isCommunityWorkout Field

## Backend (RedefinedFitness)

### Step 1: Add field to Amplify AppSync schema
- **File**: `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` as an optional field (no GSI needed — simple stored boolean)

### Step 2: Add field to Apollo Server schema
- **File**: `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to match

### Step 3: Run `pnpm generate`
- This regenerates `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` and the combined schema
- Verify the field appears in the generated TypeScript types

### Step 4: Run `pnpm typecheck`
- Ensure no type errors

## iOS (amp-ios)

### Step 5: Pull updated schema to iOS
- Copy/update `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Schema.graphql` with the new field
- Ensure `isCommunityWorkout` is included in relevant GraphQL fragments/queries

### Step 6: Add isCommunityWorkout to workout model
- Update the Swift PlannedWorkout model to include the new field
- Wire it through from the GraphQL response to the view model

### Step 7: Display community badge on workout preview
- The `LibraryBadgesView` already has a `.community` badge type in its display order
- Ensure the badge is properly shown when `isCommunityWorkout == true`
- The badge should appear on workout preview/details screens

### Step 8: Build and verify
- Run `xcodebuild` to verify compilation
- Run relevant unit/snapshot tests

## Test Plan

input.description:
## Task: Add isCommunityWorkout Boolean field to PlannedWorkout model

### What to change

1. **File: `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`**
   - Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` as an optional field on the `PlannedWorkout` type
   - Place it near other boolean fields like `isOnMachineWorkout`, `isBodyweight`, etc.
   - This is a simple stored boolean (no GSI needed), so use native `Boolean` type (NOT `BooleanValue`)

2. **File: `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`**
   - Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the Apollo Server version of the PlannedWorkout type
   - Place it in the same relative position as in the Amplify schema

3. **Also add to input types if they exist**: Check if `CreatePlannedWorkoutInput` or `UpdatePlannedWorkoutInput` are manually defined anywhere. If they're auto-generated by Amplify's `@model` directive, no manual input changes are needed.

### Important notes
- Do NOT add any GSI/index for this field
- Do NOT add any resolver for this field — it's a simple stored field
- The field should be optional (`Boolean`, not `Boolean!`)
- Follow the pattern of `isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean` which is also an optional native boolean

### Acceptance criteria
- `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` appears in both schema files
- `pnpm generate` succeeds and the field appears in `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts`
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm lint` passes (or only has pre-existing warnings)

### Verification steps (MANDATORY — run ALL and report pass/fail)
After implementation, you MUST run these steps and report pass/fail for each:
1. `pnpm generate` — MUST PASS
2. `pnpm typecheck` — MUST PASS  
3. `pnpm lint` — MUST PASS (pre-existing warnings OK)
4. Verify `isCommunityWorkout` appears in `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` by searching for it

Provide a COMPLETION REPORT at the end with pass/fail for each step.

content.text:
This is very interesting — the `isCommunityWorkout` field is **already fully wired** in the iOS app! The field exists in:
- Schema.graphql ✅
- GraphQL fragments (PlannedWorkoutBase) ✅
- Generated Swift types ✅
- PlannedWorkout model ✅
- LibraryBadgesView with `.community` badge ✅
- WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell ✅
- LibraryWorkoutCardView, LibraryWorkoutRowView ✅
- Recommendation views ✅

The iOS side already has complete support for `isCommunityWorkout`. The only thing that was missing was the **backend source schema files** — the field was in the combined schema but not in the individual source files. My backend changes fix that.

Let me verify the iOS side is truly complete by checking the badge styling.

    15	public enum BadgeStyle {
    16	    case new(withTitle: Bool)
    17	    case favorite

    19	    case plainLight(title: String)
    20	    case plainMedium(title: String)
    21	    case plainMediumLight(title: String)

    32	    case activeRecoveryDay

    34

    36	        switch self {
    37	        case let .new(withTitle):
    38	            return withTitle ? L10n.Common.new : nil
    39	        case .favorite:
    40	            return nil
    41	        case let .plain(title):

content.text:
The iOS side is **completely implemented already**. The community badge:
- Uses `.ampPerson2` icon (people icon)
- Has "Community" text label (from `L10n.Badge.community`)
- Displays in `LibraryBadgesView` when `isCommunityWorkout == true`
- Is wired through `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell`, `LibraryWorkoutCardView`, `LibraryWorkoutRowView`, and recommendation views
- The GraphQL fragment `PlannedWorkoutBase` already fetches `isCommunityWorkout`

The only missing piece was the backend source schema files, which the backend sub-agent has now fixed. Let me report iOS as complete since no changes are needed.

input.title: feat: add isCommunityWorkout field to planned workout schema
input.body:
## Description
Adds `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the PlannedWorkout source schema files (both Amplify AppSync and Apollo Server). This field marks workouts created by the Amp customer community. The field was already present in the combined schema and fully wired in the iOS app (community badge with person icon on workout preview), but was missing from the individual source schema files.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Verify `pnpm generate` succeeds with the new field
- [ ] Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] Verify community badge appears on iOS workout preview when `isCommunityWorkout` is true

input.message:
✅ Completed: *Add isCommunityWorkout field to planned workout model*

PR: https://github.com/AresFitness/RedefinedFitness/pull/3923

*What was done:*
• Added `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to both source schema files (`planned-workout.graphql` for Amplify AppSync and Apollo Server)
• Ran `pnpm generate` — field now appears in `API.ts` and all generated types (CreatePlannedWorkoutInput, UpdatePlannedWorkoutInput, PlannedWorkout type, ModelPlannedWorkoutConditionInput)
• `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` both pass

*iOS status:* No iOS changes needed — the app already has full support:
• `PlannedWorkoutBase` fragment fetches `isCommunityWorkout`
• `PlannedWorkout` Swift model has `isCommunityWorkout: Bool?`
• `LibraryBadgesView` displays a community badge (person icon + "Community" label) when `isCommunityWorkout == true`
• Badge appears on workout details header, library cards, library rows, and recommendation views

content:
No Slack thread to reply to. The task is complete. Here's a summary:

### ✅ Task Complete

**PR:** https://github.com/AresFitness/RedefinedFitness/pull/3923

#### What was done

**Backend (RedefinedFitness):**
- Added `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to both source schema files:
  - `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
  - `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Ran `pnpm generate` — field appears in `API.ts` and all generated types (CreatePlannedWorkoutInput, UpdatePlannedWorkoutInput, PlannedWorkout, ModelPlannedWorkoutConditionInput)
- `pnpm typecheck` ✅ and `pnpm lint` ✅ both pass

**iOS (amp-ios):**
- **No changes needed** — the iOS app already has complete support for `isCommunityWorkout`:
  - GraphQL fragment `PlannedWorkoutBase` fetches the field
  - `PlannedWorkout` Swift model has `isCommunityWorkout: Bool?`
  - `LibraryBadgesView` displays a **community badge** (`.ampPerson2` people icon + "Community" label) when `isCommunityWorkout == true`
  - Badge appears on workout details header, library cards, library rows, and recommendation views
